mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate at the effective lower bound compresses discount rates and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of trading months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the rank information coefficient is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0% signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the mean forward return during triggered months is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in retail sales and decline in unemployment indicate a demand-recovery narrative that resolves macro uncertainty, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of joint occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation, rising industrial production validates that output growth can absorb price pressures without severe margin compression, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: During months when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1400 thousand units signal robust residential investment momentum that raises demand for construction materials and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST exceeds 1400 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing output that reduces corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rank IC is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 250 while the federal funds rate is below 0.25, deeply negative real rates eliminate fixed-income competition and force capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL above 250 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP exceeding 17000 billion chained 2017 dollars reflects sustained aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth for large-cap corporations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly observations of GDPC1 above 17000 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.
